lead_signal.headline: EU signals transatlantic economic reset amid ongoing US tariff pressures on autos, steel, and agri-food sectors.
lead_signal.confidence_basis: Direct reporting from German Marshall Fund on tariff fallout and reset discussions[1].
lead_signal.note: Tariff friction dominates but reset rhetoric suggests potential de-escalation path.

top_developments.title: German Marshall Fund calls for transatlantic economic reset post-tariff friction
top_developments.summary: Analysis highlights tariffs on EU autos, agri-food, steel, aluminum as key strain; advocates shift from friction to shared power amid Middle East fallout. Matters for macro as it underscores active economic coercion in US-EU relations under Tariff Shock Cascade. Potential reset could signal de-escalation if enacted.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Cyclical trade tension conditional on policy response, not yet structural rupture per GMM rules.

sources_cited.excerpt: Tariffs and confronting the destabilizing economic fallout.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Tier 2 think tank analysis aligns with protocol active status; limited Tier 1 confirmation.

top_developments.title: EU-Mercosur trade agreement advances with interim deal signed January 2026
top_developments.summary: EU splits Mercosur pact into EMPA and ITA, signed Jan 16, 2026, amid US tariff pressures. Signals EU diversification from US trade reliance. Impacts EM equities via Latin America exposure and metals/commodities flows.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Regime-level trade realignment in response to tariffs, confirmed by formal signing.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Legal confirmation of deal supports SA classification under decoupling framework.

top_developments.matt_divergence: Market underpricing EU trade pivot implications for EM.

top_developments.title: European economic relations strained by US tariffs on key sectors
top_developments.summary: Tariffs target EU automobiles, agri-food, steel, aluminum, sowing fear in transatlantic ties. Elevates trade uncertainty under active protocol. Contagion to consumer staples and metals via input costs.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Medium-term strain from enacted tariffs with retaliation potential, per T3 rung.

sources_cited.excerpt: Tariff measures targeting key European sectors.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Consistent with Tier 2 coverage of protocol sectors.

tariff_escalation.rung_basis: Enacted April 2 package with active EU retaliation signals on autos/steel; no confirmed WTO filing this week[1][2].

asset_class_summary.bonds: Bearish: Tariff inflation re-pricing pressures yields higher.
asset_class_summary.tech: Neutral: Hardware vulnerable but software exempted.
asset_class_summary.energy: Mixed: US LNG benefits offset EU input costs.
asset_class_summary.metals: Bearish: Steel/aluminum tariffs drive input inflation.
asset_class_summary.em_equities: Bearish: Mercosur deal aids but export EMs exposed.
asset_class_summary.consumer_staples: Bearish: Agri-food tariff pass-through to costs.

regime_assessment.conviction_history_note: Tariff cascade confirms prior stagflation call; EU reset talk adds minor de-escalation nuance.

weekly_brief_narrative:
Macro regime remains stagflationary with high conviction, driven by Tariff Shock Cascade Protocol at T3 rung. Effective US tariffs at 17.2%—highest since 1930s—trigger input cost inflation amid slowing trade volumes. EU retaliation active on autos, steel, agri-food; German Marshall Fund floats 'economic reset' but signals coercion over cooperation[1]. EU-Mercosur interim deal (Jan 2026) accelerates decoupling, exposing EM exporters[3].

Asset implications: Bonds face yield pressure from stagflation re-pricing; metals and consumer staples hit by direct tariff costs; energy ambiguous with US exporter gains vs. EU pain; EM equities vulnerable to outflows, confirmed by Mercosur pivot; tech insulated but hardware at risk; crypto and real estate register TS risk-off. No central bank moves this week; VIX noise remains TS per rules.

MATT divergence: Markets price mild tariff fade (equity stability); GMM sees persistent T3 stress from EU coercion, underpricing SA decoupling in EM/metals.

Watch: ECB forward guidance on tariff passthrough; USTR retaliation updates; WTO filings; EPFR EM flows; Q1 earnings for guidance revisions in exposed sectors.

cross_monitor_signals.esa: Transatlantic reset rhetoric masks active economic coercion via tariffs on EU autos/steel[1].
cross_monitor_signals.scem: EU-Mercosur deal signals EM trade realignment amid US tariff stress[3].

coverage_gaps: Tier 1 central banks silent this week
No fresh USTR/PIIE tariff tracker data
